ORDER TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND (ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, DISGORGEMENT, AND QUASH SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS) DEFAULT OF MAR-10-2015 SET ASIDE

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, DISGORGEMENT, AND QUASH SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO FILE THE PROPOSED ANSWER AND CROSS-COMPLAINT THAT IS ATTACHED TO HER MOVING PAPERS BY NOVEMBER 20, 2015. PLAINTIFFS SHALL DISGORGE ALL MONEYS TAKEN FROM DEFENDANT PARUL DESAI AND PAY IN ADDITION $60.00 TO DEFENDANT FOR BANK PROCEESING FEES. SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EHG

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk KTom. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PARUL DESAI'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is off calendar. A notice of settlement of the entire case was filed on February 1, 2016. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Continued To Mar-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Disgorgement, And Quash Service Of The Summons
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY L. LICHT, TEL. NO. 1-925-483-6700 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. KLITZKE OF THE ROSNER, BARRY & BABBITT, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-858-348-1005 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PARUL DESAI'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, Disgorgement, And Quash Service Of The Summons is granted. First, there is a policy to hear cases on the merits. Second, the motion is timely per CCP 473(d). Lack of personal jurisdiction renders a default/default judgment void and it can be vacated at any time. See Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249. Third, defendant's reply declaration, which is signed, establishes that she resided in Canada at the time plaintiffs attempted to effectuate service on January 24, 2015. Defendant has leave to file the proposed answer and cross-complaint that is attached to her moving papers by November 20, 2015. Plaintiffs shall disgorge all moneys taken from Defendant Parul Desai and pay in addition $60.00 to Defendant for bank proceesing fees. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
